notes: Aberdeen's term for constraint-based planning/scheduling systems; evolved into modern APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) and S&OP software.
First-generation manufacturing planning; criticized for infinite capacity assumptions and inability to optimize. APET positioned as successor.
Second-generation MRP; APET positioned to replace MRP II modules for master scheduling and capacity planning.
i2's flagship APET product line including Master Planner, Factory Planner, Distribution Planner, Scheduler, Forecast Planner. Acquired by JDA 2010.
Agent-based APET using in-memory optimization; acquired by PeopleSoft 1996 for $225M; became foundation of PeopleSoft Manufacturing module.
Client/server APET with Synchronizer, Coordinator, Operator, Optimizer modules.
Integrated demand planning, manufacturing planning, transportation planning, distribution planning. Acquired by JDA 2006.
Five modules including Linx, Planx, Schedulex, SCI/SCV, 3D (Dynamic Deployment & Distribution).
Supply Chain Planning, Visual Planning Workstation, Flow Manufacturing suite. Still active as Logility.
The broader category that APET belongs to; became standard enterprise software category by late 1990s.
APET systems deployed as client/server using Windows PCs and UNIX servers with RDBMS backends.
Many APET products designed using OO technology for improved usability and flexibility.
